market_pulse.one_liner: Dealer-stabilized bullish regime masking extreme volatility fragility and sentiment divergence.

gamma_landscape.notes: Dealer gamma is net positive, providing a floor at 730, but the 170:1 VEX-to-GEX ratio indicates that any volatility spike will trigger rapid dealer hedging and liquidity evaporation.

risks: Extreme VEX concentration (1.05B) suggests high sensitivity to sudden volatility shocks.
Geopolitical escalation in Ukraine/Romania is currently ignored by the market.
Severe divergence between retail euphoria (WSB) and institutional caution (Burry/r/stocks).
Low model accuracy (20-41%) indicates high probability of signal failure.
disagreements: Experts diverged on the sustainability of the 'risk-on' regime; quantitative models emphasize dealer support, while contrarian/fundamental analysts highlight the extreme risk of a sentiment-driven mean reversion.
Disagreement on the validity of current long signals given the poor historical accuracy of the model stack.
narrative: The market is currently pinned by dealer gamma at the 730 level, creating a false sense of stability that ignores significant geopolitical and fundamental risks. While retail sentiment remains aggressively bullish, the extreme volatility exposure and institutional skepticism suggest that the current 'risk-on' environment is highly fragile and prone to a sharp, sentiment-driven unwind.
